title: Our approach to survey compliance | Stats NZ
content:
## Our approach to survey compliance
The information our surveys provide is so important to New Zealand that taking part is a legal requirement. Most people are happy to take part, especially when they understand the value of what they’re contributing to. We also try to make the process as easy as possible.
The model below describes our approach to survey compliance.
Figure 1: Our approach to survey compliance
Stats NZ is committed to helping people meet their obligations by making compliance as easy as possible. We want to do all we can to encourage and help survey participants to comply with their obligation to complete surveys under the Data and Statistics Act 2022.
Our four core actions (as shown in the centre of the model) are described below.
1. Inform – by making sure you have the right information, at the right time – information about who we are, why we do what we do, and what it means for you.
Approaches to support this:
- Build awareness of Stats NZ.
- Show the value of what we do.
- Build compliance from the start.
2. Motivate – by highlighting the value of contribution and the information produced, so that people are internally motivated to take part.
Approaches to support this:
- Build respectful relationships.
- Acknowledge value of contribution.
3. Facilitate – by helping those who find it difficult to take part, for whatever reason (and acknowledging that sometimes participation may not be possible).
Approaches to support this:
- Identify and manage burden.
- Seek feedback and improve.
4. Enforce – by identifying those few who have decided not to do their share, and applying the law fairly but firmly.
Stats NZ surveys are a request under section 23 of the Data and Statistics Act 2022. Everyone who is asked to complete the survey needs to do so by the end of the collection period. Under section 76 of the Act, failing to complete the survey is an offence; conviction could lead to a fine of up to $2,000 for an individual or $12,000 for a business or organisation.
Approach to support this:
The universal messages complete the model:
- Information is so important, it's a legal requirement.
- Maintaining relationships is key to maintaining the supply of data.
- Survey participation is crucial to growing the data that informs decisions, improving lives today and for generations to come.
This approach to survey compliance reinforces the commitments we’ve made in our survey charter.
Stats NZ survey charter gives you more detail about what you can expect from us, how you can help us, and what to do if you have any complaints, concerns, or other feedback.
Page updated 20 September 2023
